stem: The expression 4x2 − 73x + 18 can be written in the form (4x − r)(x − s), where r and s are positive integers. What is the value of r + s?
rationale: We need positive integers r and s with rs = 18 and r + 4s = 73. Trying s = 18, r = 1: r + 4s = 1 + 72 = 73 ✓ and rs = 18 ✓. So (4x − 1)(x − 18) expands to 4x2 − 73x + 18. Therefore r + s = 1 + 18 = 19. The other choices arise from misfactoring (using r + s = 27 from s = 9, r = 3 fails the linear-term check, etc.).
